input.snippet:
FROM nvidia/cuda:12.4.1-runtime-ubuntu22.04
RUN pip install vllm torch

expected.finding: The repository uses NVIDIA-specific container or runtime settings that will not run cleanly on AMD ROCm infrastructure.
expected.recommendation: Add a ROCm-specific runtime profile using a ROCm/vLLM image and move NVIDIA launch flags behind backend-specific scripts.

expected.finding: The repository uses NVIDIA-specific container or runtime settings that will not run cleanly on AMD ROCm infrastructure.
expected.recommendation: Add a ROCm-specific runtime profile using a ROCm/vLLM image and move NVIDIA launch flags behind backend-specific scripts.

expected.recommendation: Add a ROCm-specific runtime profile using a ROCm/vLLM image and move NVIDIA launch flags behind backend-specific scripts.

expected.recommendation: Introduce a device resolver that treats HIP-backed torch.cuda availability as ROCm and logs backend provenance.

conversation: Repo Doctor found nvidia-smi in scripts/healthcheck.sh.
Build Runner said this blocks AMD proof because rocm-smi or amd-smi is needed.
Report Agent must not claim live AMD validation until logs are captured.

expected.memory_write: Store that NVIDIA monitoring commands must be replaced with AMD SMI evidence before the report claims ROCm validation.
expected.reuse_rule: Recall this memory in later reports or patch plans when the same blocker appears.

conversation: Migration Planner proposed deleting CUDA support entirely.
Build Runner objected because maintainers need dual-backend support.
Orchestrator chose separate ROCm files and profiles.

expected.memory_write: Store that ROCmPilot should preserve CUDA as an optional backend and add separate ROCm paths instead of destructive rewrites.
expected.reuse_rule: Recall this memory in later reports or patch plans when the same blocker appears.

conversation: Benchmark Agent saw estimated MI300X numbers.
Report Agent asked how to label them.
Consensus: estimates are static profiles until AMD Developer Cloud logs exist.

expected.memory_write: Store that estimated metrics must be labeled as static ROCmPilot profiles and replaced by live AMD logs when available.
expected.reuse_rule: Recall this memory in later reports or patch plans when the same blocker appears.

input.question: Why does torch.cuda still appear on ROCm?

expected.answer: Explain that HIP-backed PyTorch intentionally exposes much of the torch.cuda API on ROCm, so the agent should inspect torch.version.hip, package wheels, container base image, and runtime logs before treating every torch.cuda call as a blocker.
expected.must_do: Separate API compatibility from NVIDIA-only assumptions.
expected.tone: direct, helpful, and honest about proof boundaries

input.question: Should I delete CUDA support when adding ROCm?

expected.answer: Recommend dual-backend support: preserve CUDA paths, add ROCm profiles, and make backend selection explicit through config, scripts, or containers.
expected.must_do: Avoid destructive migration advice.
expected.tone: direct, helpful, and honest about proof boundaries

expected.diagnosis: The droplet or vLLM server is down, blocked by firewall, or not listening on the expected port.
expected.fix: Verify the droplet is running, check docker ps, inspect vLLM logs, test /v1/models locally, then test the public URL.
expected.fallback_rule: If AMD fails, use Hugging Face before deterministic fallback and label the source truthfully.

input.status: AMD endpoint times out then HF works

expected.diagnosis: Fallback is behaving correctly; the AMD path is unavailable or too slow for the report timeout.
expected.fix: Keep HF fallback active for demos and only claim AMD-connected reports after the Report Agent source is amd-vllm.
expected.fallback_rule: If AMD fails, use Hugging Face before deterministic fallback and label the source truthfully.

input.status: vLLM loads but OOMs on first request

expected.diagnosis: The serving profile is too aggressive for the selected model length, batch shape, or GPU memory utilization.
expected.fix: Reduce max model length, lower gpu memory utilization, use a smaller Qwen model, and capture the new startup logs.
expected.fallback_rule: If AMD fails, use Hugging Face before deterministic fallback and label the source truthfully.
